goal: Add a contract-only provider/runtime activation preflight based on Maya-Core gate evidence, without calling providers, executing runtime, writing state, or opening runtime routes.
scope_out: Do not call providers, execute runtime, mount runtime execution, call Maya-Core from this route, write DB/files/GitHub, issue permits, merge, deploy, or change package/workflow files.
claims: Bluepilot can dry-preflight provider activation from Maya budget/cost evidence.
Bluepilot can dry-preflight runtime dry-run activation from Maya budget/corridor evidence and runtime decision evidence.
Even when review-ready, providerActivationAllowed, runtimeActivationAllowed, and dryRunRouteMountAllowed remain false.
assumptions: Actual provider calls and runtime execution require later explicit activation locks.
Maya-Core live reachability remains isolated to existing health/gate surfaces.
dod: GET /probe/provider-runtime-activation-contract returns the contract-only activation boundary.
POST /probe/provider-runtime-activation-preflight validates provider and runtime dry-run evidence without execution.
The eight-point readiness and operator dashboard mark Provider and Runtime Flows as wired_contract_only.
Focused tests, typecheck, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: A provider call, runtime execution, runtime route mount, Maya-Core call from the new route, DB write, file write, GitHub write, permit issue, merge, deploy, package change, or workflow change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Maya-Core Gate Enforcement is now wired_contract_only and can dry-validate evidence for provider, write, and runtime activation review.
